notes.content: The European Studies major consists of 6 preparation courses, the language requirement, and 12 Upper Division classes. Students in the major have options to study abroad, participate in research, and complete internships associated with their major. Some students choose to double-major with other programs on campus, and several choose to add a minor. Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major. To be considered for this major, you must complete the following preparatory courses by the end of spring before transfer: One year of a college level modern European language OR At least one year of high school in which the primary language of instruction was a modern European language, and One course from: History of Europe, French Literature; Russian Film, French Culture/Civilization, Russian Literature; two courses in sociocultural anthropology, contemporary world history, cultural geography, world literature and two courses in comparative politics, introductory sociology, economic geography and macroeconomics and microeconomics (in addition to foreign language). Completion of intermediate/advanced foreign language is STRONGLY RECOMMENDED . All courses must be taken for a letter grade. For more information regarding preparation for this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://www.international.ucla.edu/esba and https://admission.ucla.edu . PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses.
notes.contentHtml:
The European Studies major consists of 6 preparation courses, the language requirement, and 12 Upper Division classes. Students in the major have options to study abroad, participate in research, and complete internships associated with their major. Some students choose to double-major with other programs on campus, and several choose to add a minor.


Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major.


To be considered for this major, you must complete the following preparatory courses by the end of spring before transfer:

  • One year of a college level modern European language OR
  • At least one year of high school in which the primary language of instruction was a modern European language, and
  • One course from: History of Europe, French Literature; Russian Film, French Culture/Civilization, Russian Literature; two courses in sociocultural anthropology, contemporary world history, cultural geography, world literature and two courses in comparative politics, introductory sociology, economic geography and macroeconomics and microeconomics (in addition to foreign language).

Completion of intermediate/advanced foreign language is STRONGLY RECOMMENDED.

All courses must be taken for a letter grade.


For more information regarding preparation for this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://www.international.ucla.edu/esba and https://admission.ucla.edu.


PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses.

notes.content: Two years of a modern foreign language other than English. The language requirement can also be fulfilled in part or in total by taking a placement examination given through the appropriate language department at UCLA.
notes.contentHtml: Two years of a modern foreign language other than English.  The language requirement can also be fulfilled in part or in total by taking a placement examination given through the appropriate language department at UCLA.

notes.content: The AP Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the major prep: A score of 4-5 on the AP Microeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 1 (if taken before spring 2021) and can be used in place of the microeconomics course. A score of 5 on the AP Microeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 1 (if taken after spring 2021) and can be used in place of the microeconomics course. A score of 4-5 on the AP Macroeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 2 (if taken before spring 2021) and can be used in place of the macroeconomics course. A score of 5 on the AP Macroeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 2 (if taken after spring 2021) and can be used in place of the macroeconomics course. A score of 3 on the AP French Language and Culture exam is equivalent to FRNCH 4. A score of 4 on the AP French Language and Culture exam is equivalent to FRNCH 5. A score of 5 on the AP French Language and Culture exam is equivalent to FRNCH 6. A score of 3 on the AP German Language and Culture exam is equivalent to GERMAN 3. A score of 4 on the AP German Language and Culture exam is equivalent to GERMAN 4. A score of 5 on the AP German Language and Culture exam is equivalent to GERMAN 5. A score of 3 on the AP Italian Language and Culture exam is equivalent to ITALIAN 3. A score of 4 on the AP Italian Language and Culture exam is equivalent to ITALIAN 4. A score of 5 on the AP Italian Language and Culture exam is equivalent to ITALIAN 6. A score of 3 or higher on the AP Comparative Government exam can be used in place of the comparative politics course (POL SCI 50).
notes.contentHtml:
The AP Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the major prep:

  • A score of 4-5 on the AP Microeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 1 (if taken before spring 2021) and can be used in place of the microeconomics course.
  • A score of 5 on the AP Microeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 1 (if taken after spring 2021) and can be used in place of the microeconomics course.
  • A score of 4-5 on the AP Macroeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 2 (if taken before spring 2021) and can be used in place of the macroeconomics course.
  • A score of 5 on the AP Macroeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 2 (if taken after spring 2021) and can be used in place of the macroeconomics course.
  • A score of 3 on the AP French Language and Culture exam is equivalent to FRNCH 4.
  • A score of 4 on the AP French Language and Culture exam is equivalent to FRNCH 5.
  • A score of 5 on the AP French Language and Culture exam is equivalent to FRNCH 6.
  • A score of 3 on the AP German Language and Culture exam is equivalent to GERMAN 3.
  • A score of 4 on the AP German Language and Culture exam is equivalent to GERMAN 4.
  • A score of 5 on the AP German Language and Culture exam is equivalent to GERMAN 5.
  • A score of 3 on the AP Italian Language and Culture exam is equivalent to ITALIAN 3.
  • A score of 4 on the AP Italian Language and Culture exam is equivalent to ITALIAN 4.
  • A score of 5 on the AP Italian Language and Culture exam is equivalent to ITALIAN 6.
  • A score of 3 or higher on the AP Comparative Government exam can be used in place of the comparative politics course (POL SCI 50).
